articles.title: TESLA'S GRAPHICS ARE ABOUT TO GET UNREAL
articles.summary: Tesla's in-car visualizations for Autopilot and Full Self-Driving may switch to Epic Games' Unreal Engine based on firmware evidence. Tesla hacker greentheonly discovered signs of this change in the 2025.20 firmware for Model S and X vehicles with AMD chips. Other automakers, including Rivian, Ford, and Volvo, already use Unreal Engine as Epic pushes into automotive applications.

articles.title: FASTER ANIMATIONS ON IOS 26 MAKES EVEN OLDER IPHONES FEEL LIKE NEW
articles.summary: iOS 26 introduces faster app launch animations with a subtle warp effect, making apps reach near full-screen about 150 milliseconds quicker and creating the impression of a snappier, more responsive iPhone. While actual performance is unchanged, the speedier pacing—similar to Android's—enhances perceived performance, boosting user satisfaction, especially on high-refresh-rate displays.

articles.title: DECART RAISES $100M ON $3.1B VALUATION TO GROW REAL-TIME AI VIDEO PLATFORM
articles.summary: Decart raised $100 million on a $3.1 billion valuation to expand its real-time AI video platform technology. The company's GPU optimization breakthrough reduces video generation costs from hundreds of dollars per hour to under 25 cents. Decart's products, Oasis and MirageLSD, enable real-time video generation for gaming, entertainment, and interactive applications.

articles.title: BEING A DESIGNER USED TO FEEL LIKE A CAREER—NOW IT JUST FEELS LIKE A JOB
articles.summary: Design has transformed from a meaningful career into just a job as modern workflows stripped away its creative essence. Designers ironically built the very systems, design frameworks, templates, and AI tools that automated and sanitized their work while deluding themselves about design's world-changing power. Reclaiming design as a career requires small acts of resistance within existing systems, defending creative choices, and maintaining authorship rather than defaulting to safe, predictable options.

articles.title: ON TEAMWORK
articles.summary: This reflection on one year at Atlassian explores what makes great teams across sports, tech, and fiction. Five key attributes are identified: high trust, identity, shared intuition, shorthand communication, and mission-driven purpose. Atlassian Design's strength lies in its shared belief that it can become the best team.

articles.title: WHERE HAVE ALL THE JUNIOR DESIGNERS GONE?
articles.summary: Only 13% of nearly 100 recent graphic design job listings were entry-level, with most seeking senior-level skills from the start. Companies are increasingly replacing junior roles with internships—often unpaid or underpaid—leaving newcomers in cycles of temporary work or unsustainable freelancing without clear career progression.

articles.title: CURATED LIBRARY OF OPEN GRAPH IMAGES
articles.summary: OGFolio is a curated gallery of high-quality Open Graph images from top websites. Browse by theme, color, or industry to get inspired and level up your link previews.

articles.title: TURN SCREENSHOTS INTO WORKS OF ART
articles.summary: Shotva helps you create beautiful, shareable images from your screenshots with custom backgrounds, frames, annotations, and AI-powered branding.

articles.title: HOW I APPROACH MOTION IN PRODUCT DESIGN
articles.summary: A product designer outlines their systematic approach to motion design, highlighting how animation critically shapes product feel, guides user attention, and reinforces brand personality. Core principles include defaulting to ease-out curves, matching animation timing to element size, using faster exit transitions, and embracing spring animations for natural interactions. The framework emphasizes custom animation curves, motion libraries for enhanced capabilities, and purposeful restraint to avoid overwhelming users with excessive animation.

articles.title: APPLE'S LIQUID GLASS SHATTERS TYPOGRAPHY
articles.summary: Apple's iOS 26 introduces a Liquid Glass UI design that prioritizes aesthetics over readability of typography. The interface suffers from poor color contrast, fragile customization options, and buried accessibility features. Designers should prioritize robust text contrast and accessibility-first design over trendy transparency effects.

articles.title: MACOS TAHOE ADDS 15 BEAUTIFUL NEW SCREENSAVERS, AVAILABLE HERE
articles.summary: macOS Tahoe, launching next month, brings 15 new screensavers—ranging from Tahoe landscapes to Himalayan peaks and Goa beaches—available now via Apple's direct links. This larger-than-usual collection stems from tvOS 26's expanded screensaver set, with the videos sourced directly from Apple's servers.

articles.title: ROI OF UX: WHY CFOS AND CEOS NEED TO PRIORITISE DESIGN
articles.summary: Strong UX delivers measurable business gains with companies outperforming industry averages, yet many CEOs and CFOs continue to undervalue design's revenue impact.

articles.title: THE DESIGN FLAW HOLDING BACK TODAY'S HUMANOID ROBOTS
articles.summary: Current humanoid robots suffer from a fundamental design flaw: rigid bodies with limited joints that contrast with natural, compliant movement.

articles.title: FUNKY NEW WALLPAPER TO WELCOME THE LATEST APPLE STORE
articles.summary: Apple will open its new Qianhai Yifang City retail store in Shenzhen, which features the brand's signature glass-heavy mall design and a special downloadable wallpaper to mark the occasion, on August 16.
